market_notes: ALPHA GENERATION: Portfolio +10.8% total P&L vs SPY +3.3% 1M, QQQ +3.5% 1M, SMH +14.2% 1M — generating strong alpha. TSM reports April 16 — Q1 preliminary revenue +35% confirmed; holding 9 shares. IRAN STRAIT OF HORMUZ BLOCKADE now underway per IBD — this materially strengthens the LNG and energy thesis; LNG position essentially at stop ($261.60 vs $261.28 stop) — may auto-trigger but thesis has strengthened dramatically. AI capex supercycle intact: MRVL hitting new records daily (Amazon custom ASIC), AVGO reigniting (Barron's), NVDA recovering. Goldman Sachs warns fears of disruption will hang over growth stocks for years — backdrop caution but not thesis-breaking. Nasdaq 9 consecutive up days. ASML earnings April 15 tomorrow — key semiconductor equipment cycle indicator; watching for weakness as entry opportunity. Cash at ~12% is strategic ahead of earnings deployments.
watching: TSM earnings April 16 — HOLDING 9 shares at +8.2%; watch CoWoS capacity guidance and 2026 capex outlook; consider small add if weakness on guidance but Q1 already confirmed +35% beat
ASML earnings April 15 — not held; watching for post-earnings weakness as entry at ~33x fwd P/E for EUV monopoly; Ken Fisher top pick; China export curb risk could create buying opportunity

LNG — 10 shares essentially at stop ($261.60 vs $261.28 stop); Iran Strait of Hormuz blockade is BULLISH catalyst — if stop triggers, watch for re-entry as blockade thesis plays out; EQT signing long-term LNG agreements confirms structural demand
MRVL — 40 shares at +38.4% unrealized; hitting new records daily on Amazon custom ASIC; 49.8% 1M run creates crowding risk but Amazon catalyst is fresh; next trim target at all-time high breach
AVGO — 37 shares at +25.7% unrealized; Barron's 'reigniting'; IBD big winner; CoreWeave/Meta deals; HOLD core position as hyperscaler ASIC thesis accelerates
PANW — 20 shares at -3.9% unrealized; stop $143.62; Zacks/Barchart asking 'buy the dip'; platform consolidation thesis intact; watch for stabilization and potential add
EQIX — 8 shares at +9.7% unrealized; Morgan Stanley now bullish on AI inference demand; fwd P/E 60x still stretched but thesis strengthening; HOLD
GLW — 15 shares at +18.4% unrealized; 35% 1M run; Motley Fool 'Is It a Buy in 2026?' article suggests retail is noticing; at 45x fwd P/E watch for trim opportunity near all-time high

Post-TSM earnings deployment — ASML weakness, TSM add on weakness, potential new position in ADI (fwd P/E 26.8x, 30% rev growth) or MU (fwd P/E 4.3x, 196% rev growth — extremely cheap on forward basis)
Iran Hormuz blockade escalation — positive for LNG, EQT, HAL, XOM; watch for energy infrastructure names to strengthen; consider LNG re-entry if stop triggers and blockade sustains

recent_runs.reasoning: **Macro:** The market is showing tentative stabilization after Q1 turbulence — SPY -0.2% 1W, QQQ -0.6% 1W, SMH -1.8% 1W, all improved from the deep selloffs of late March. Iran war-exit signals continue to reduce geopolitical risk premium, crude retreating further, and the AI capex cycle remains structurally intact with OpenAI's $122B raise at $852B valuation, NVIDIA's continued ecosystem investment ($2B in MRVL, Lumentum, Synopsys, Nokia deals per news), and JPM's $3.8B AI data center bond that drew $14B in orders. Semiconductor tariff carveouts remain pending but possible — a positive asymmetric catalyst. Earnings season begins this week with financials (JPM, GS, C, MS on April 13-15) providing a macro health read-through. **Sectors:** AI infrastructure (semiconductors, data centers, networking) remains the dominant multi-year theme — MRVL +37.7% 1M, CIEN +24.7% 1M, ARM +27.4% 1M showing exceptional relative strength even in a choppy tape. Energy is giving back Iran risk premium but LNG's structural European demand thesis remains intact (Qatar LNG shock analysis positive for Cheniere per recent coverage). Cybersecurity (PANW +4.9% 1W, CRWD +1.9% 1W) and defensive consumer (COST +2.2% 1W, TJX +1.4% 1W) continuing to hold up well. **Positions:** Portfolio total P&L now +4.3% ($4,303) — strong outperformance vs benchmarks (SPY -3.4% 1M, QQQ -2.8% 1M). All 13 positions are above their stops. Key observations: (1) MRVL is the star at +12.5% unrealized — Bank of America raised PT on NVDA partnership validation; at -1.1% from 52W high, it is near all-time highs with the thesis fully intact. (2) META +10.2% unrealized at $579 — strong performer, swing trader alignment (both long). (3) ARM +7.6% at $155 — Composite Rating rose to 96 per IBD, Jim Cramer calling it 'one of the most important companies in semiconductors.' (4) LNG -7.1% from cost at $275.84 — this is the weakest position with Iran peace deal signals reducing risk premium, but Qatar LNG shock analysis (Simply Wall St.) supports structural thesis; stop at $261.28 remains ~$14 below current price. (5) CVX -6.5% from cost — energy retreating but FCF generation remains strong at current oil prices. (6) CIEN -5.1% 1W this week but +3.4% unrealized; WaveLogic 6 deployment with Vodafone Idea is a positive catalyst. Cash at $5,162 (~5%) limits new additions without trimming. No stops are under immediate threat. The portfolio is well-constructed with strong conviction across all positions. No trades warranted this session — patience remains the correct posture as earnings season approaches.

**Macro:** The market remains in a stabilization phase post-Q1 turbulence — SPY -0.2% 1W, QQQ -0.6% 1W, SMH -1.8% 1W — consistent with last session's read. Iran war-exit signals continue to reduce geopolitical risk premium (crude retreating second consecutive session), which is a headwind for LNG/CVX but a tailwind for the broad market. AI capex cycle structurally intact: OpenAI raised $122B at $852B valuation, JPM's $3.8B AI data center bond drew $14B in orders, and NVIDIA's ecosystem investments continue accelerating. Earnings season begins this week with financials (JPM April 14, GS April 13, C April 14, MS April 15) providing a macro health read-through, and TSM reports April 16 — the most important semiconductor earnings of the season. **Sectors:** AI infrastructure (semiconductors, data centers, networking) remains the dominant multi-year theme — MRVL +37.7% 1M, CIEN +24.7% 1M, ARM +27.4% 1M showing exceptional relative strength. Energy names (LNG -3.0% 1W, CVX -3.8% 1W) are giving back Iran premium but structural theses remain intact. PANW +4.9% 1W with CEO buying $10M personally — cybersecurity secular demand intact. TSM at fwd P/E 19x, -12.2% from 52W high, 45% margins, 35% ROE remains my highest-conviction watch candidate. **Positions:** Portfolio +4.3% total P&L, outperforming all three benchmarks. MRVL at +12.5% unrealized is the standout — at -1.1% from 52W high, near peak valuation territory. Bank of America raised PT on NVDA NVLink partnership. MRVL has now run 37.7% in one month — the risk/reward is becoming less asymmetric at current prices. META +10.2% at $579 remains strong. LNG -7.1% from cost with Iran deal signals reducing energy premium — the structural thesis (Qatar LNG shock, European demand) remains but near-term price pressure is real with stop at $261.28 still ~$14 below current. CVX -6.5% from cost; stop at $185.81 provides protection. Cash at $5,162 (~5%) limits new additions. Key action to consider: trim MRVL to raise cash for TSM ahead of its April 16 earnings — TSM is the world's irreplaceable semiconductor manufacturer at a genuinely attractive valuation (fwd P/E 19x), and the swing trader is already long TSM confirming the thesis. MRVL is approaching all-time highs with the thesis fully priced in. Trimming 25-30 shares of MRVL (~$3,200) would fund a 9-share TSM position (~$3,073) while improving portfolio diversification and reducing concentration in a name that has already captured 37.7% in a month.

**Macro:** The market is recovering — SPY +1.7% 1W, QQQ +1.9% 1W, SMH +3.0% 1W — with the first weekly gain for SPY since February. Iran tensions are escalating again per Trump's latest threats (oil spiking), which creates a bifurcated environment: energy names recovering while tech faces pressure from oil-driven macro fears. AI capex cycle remains intact — NVIDIA's Jensen Huang commented on 'astounding' revenue potential; the AI infrastructure buildout is accelerating. Earnings season begins Monday with GS/JPM/C/MS all reporting April 13-15, and TSM reporting April 16 — critical catalysts for our portfolio. **Sectors:** AI infrastructure remains the dominant long-term theme — SMH +3.0% 1W outperforming, MRVL +9.7% 1W and +37.2% 1M (now -0.7% from 52W highs), CIEN +15.4% 1W and +30.3% 1M (now -1.2% from 52W high), ARM +20.1% 1M. Energy is bifurcated — oil spiking on Iran threats is positive for CVX/LNG structurally but creates macro headwinds for the broader market. PANW +4.4% 1W with CEO $10M personal buy continues to be compelling. Wall Street upgraded LNG on Iran reshaping global LNG demand — positive thesis confirmation. The CIEN valuation concern is now real at fwd P/E 54x with the stock +30% in a month and -1.2% from 52W high. **Positions:** Portfolio at +4.1% total P&L ($4,143) — outperforming SPY (-4.0% 1M), QQQ (-4.1% 1M), SMH (-1.7% 1M). Key observations: (1) MRVL is now -0.7% from 52W HIGH at $107.11 — extremely extended, +37.2% 1M; the stock has fully captured the NVIDIA validation thesis. Risk/reward at current levels with fwd P/E 19.8x is less compelling given the run. Considering a trim of 15-20 shares (~$1,600-$2,100) to bank profits and raise cash ahead of earnings season. (2) CIEN is +11.5% unrealized, +15.4% 1W this week, now at fwd P/E 54x and -1.2% from 52W high — the optical networking thesis is valid but valuation is stretched. A partial trim (2-3 shares) locks in gains while maintaining exposure. (3) CVX -5.8% from cost and oil spiking on Iran threats — this actually supports the CVX thesis; the Iran escalation is a positive for energy names. Hold. (4) LNG -5.3% from cost but Wall Street just upgraded it on Iran-driven LNG demand reshaping — Iran conflict is POSITIVE for LNG thesis, not negative as previously feared. The 'Iran war exit' narrative from last week appears to have reversed. LNG stop at $261.28 still intact. (5) TSM reports April 16 — current price $339.04, -0.7% from cost. 24/7 Wall St. article asks 'How Big Will Taiwan Semiconductor's Beat Be?' — expectations are high. I will hold through earnings given the strong thesis and the TSMC Japan 3nm approval being a positive catalyst. (6) PANW — CEO bought $10M personally, +4.4% 1W, now at $163.21, -27% from 52W high. With cash at $4,756 (~4.5%), I don't have capacity to add PANW without trimming something. Given MRVL and CIEN being at near-term valuation extremes, trimming to fund PANW makes sense. Action plan: SELL 15 MRVL (bank some of the +12.9% gain at near 52W highs) and SELL 2 CIEN (valuation stretched at 54x fwd) to raise ~$2,500 in cash, then BUY 5 PANW at $163. This improves portfolio diversification into cybersecurity while trimming two names at/near 52W highs. MRVL retains 65-share position (still ~7% weight) — thesis intact but position de-risked. CIEN retains 4-share position (~$1,800) — still meaningful exposure.

**Macro:** The macro backdrop has shifted more constructively — SPY +1.7% 1W marks the first weekly gain since February, Iran tensions remain elevated (oil above $100, Trump threats continuing) which supports energy names, and the AI capex cycle remains robust with NVIDIA ecosystem investments accelerating. Earnings season is the dominant near-term catalyst with GS April 13, JPM/JNJ/C April 14, MS/PNC/ASML April 15, TSM/NFLX/SCHW/ABT/PLD April 16. **Sectors:** AI infrastructure (semiconductors, data centers, networking) continues to be the strongest multi-year secular theme — MRVL +37.2% 1M at -0.7% from 52W high is the most extended name; CIEN +30.3% 1M at -1.2% from 52W high similarly stretched but thesis intact. Energy bifurcated: Iran escalation positive for CVX/LNG structurally but oil stocks may be 'tapped out' per Barron's. GLW +9.3% 1W on $6B Meta AI fiber deal is an interesting pick-and-shovels play. ASML earnings April 15 is critical leading indicator for the semiconductor equipment cycle — House bill would ban more chipmaking equipment to China adds headline risk. **Positions:** Portfolio at +3.9% total P&L ($3,868) vs SPY -4.0% 1M — meaningful alpha generation. Key considerations: (1) MRVL at -0.7% from 52W high after +37.2% 1M — at this level risk/reward is less attractive; the NVIDIA $2B investment thesis is fully priced in; however, the fwd P/E 19.8x is not extreme for the AI custom silicon growth story; HOLD at current size. (2) TSM earnings April 16 — 9 shares at -0.7% from cost; multiple positive signals (Billionaire Steve Cohen buying, 'Strong AI Chip Demand' thesis intact, 3nm Japan fab approval); will hold through earnings per plan. (3) CIEN at -1.2% from 52W high with 285x trailing P/E and 54x fwd P/E — extremely stretched; however only 4 shares remain after prior trimming; the AI networking demand story (GLW $6B Meta deal confirms) supports the thesis; HOLD 4 shares. (4) PANW at $163.21 — CEO $10M buy thesis intact, cybersecurity secular theme, -27% from 52W high; only 5 shares at ~0.8% weight — consider building this position. (5) AVGO — Billionaire Steve Cohen 'almost doubles stake' per news; DISA $970M BPA contract announced; fwd P/E 17.6x is very attractive for the custom silicon thesis; current 33 shares at +4.6% unrealized. (6) GLW — $6B Meta AI fiber deal announced this week is a major catalyst; +9.3% 1W; optical fiber is a critical picks-and-shovels beneficiary of AI data center buildout; -8.6% from 52W high at attractive 37.9x fwd P/E; worth a starter position. (7) LLY — FDA approved Foundayo weight management pill this week; NVO pricing shift unlocking larger obesity market; LLY at -17.4% from 52W high, fwd P/E 22.2x for 43% revenue growth is genuinely attractive; one of the highest-quality businesses in healthcare. No positions are near stop losses. Cash at $6,443 (~6.2% of ~$104K portfolio) gives room for 1-2 small additions. I will add GLW as a new AI fiber infrastructure position and add to PANW to build toward target weight, funded by current cash position. These are both high-conviction adds that improve diversification into optical networking and cybersecurity.

Key actions to consider: (1) AVGO: The Google long-term AI chip deal just announced today is a thesis-confirming mega-catalyst. This materially de-risks the thesis and strengthens the growth runway. With 33 shares at 4.6% unrealized P&L, I want to add to this position — it is now the highest-conviction name in the portfolio with multiple hyperscaler relationships confirmed and a fwd P/E of 17.6x that remains compelling. TSM Thursday — my most important earnings event; holding 9 shares; expecting beat on AI chip demand. The AI capex cycle remains the dominant structural force with Dimon flagging $725B AI capital spending in 2026 and Anthropic launching new cybersecurity AI partnerships benefiting PANW and CRWD. EARNINGS ALERTS: GS reporting today — not held but watching; financial sector health is positive read-through. ARM downgrade is the most actionable event today. Trimming ARM reduces concentration risk in an expensive name facing momentum headwinds; trimming MRVL further on insider selling de-risks the extended winner; proceeds fund PANW addition toward target weight. **Cash:** Cash at $3,322 (~3.2%) is near the floor — trimming ARM and MRVL generates capacity to add PANW and keeps cash above the 1% floor while rebalancing risk toward the strongest near-term catalysts.

**Macro:** The Iran ceasefire (Trump suspending attacks for 2 weeks, Vance leading negotiations) drove a massive Dow +1,300 point rally today with oil prices plunging — risk-on conditions have returned sharply. The AI capex cycle remains the dominant structural force with BofA hiking 2026 chip revenue forecast to $1.3T and naming NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, and AMD as top drivers. The ceasefire is a near-term headwind for energy names (CVX -6.8% 1W, LNG -3.0% 1W) but a broad market tailwind. **Sectors:** Semiconductors are the clear winner — SMH +10.3% 1W, MRVL +15.6% 1W at -1.1% from 52W high, AMAT +12.8% 1W, AMD +14.0% 1W, LRCX +15.4% 1W — AI infrastructure buildout is accelerating with BofA's $1.3T chip revenue forecast confirming the multi-year thesis. Optical networking (CIEN +27.3% 1W now at -1.1% from 52W high at 59.8x fwd P/E, GLW +21.4% 1W now -0.1% from 52W high) is extremely extended. Cybersecurity is strengthening (PANW +8.4% 1W on Anthropic partnership). Energy is the clear sector to reduce exposure as Iran ceasefire removes the risk premium that was supporting CVX and LNG. **Positions:** Portfolio at +7.3% total P&L ($7,299) — strong alpha vs SPY -0.1% 1M, QQQ -0.1% 1M. Key issues: (1) CVX: -8.6% unrealized and oil is now plunging on ceasefire — the Iran risk premium that supported the energy thesis is actively deflating. CVX at fwd P/E 18.9x with -8% revenue growth is no longer compelling as a portfolio holding given energy sector headwinds; the stop at $185.81 is only $7.08 away from current price $192.89 — approaching stop territory; considering a proactive exit to redeploy capital. (2) LNG: -7.3% unrealized, -3.0% 1W, stop at $261.28 with current price $275.21 — $13.93 above stop; ceasefire removes near-term Iran risk premium but Goldman raised PT 11% and take-or-pay contracts provide structural floor; HOLD but monitoring closely. (3) EQIX: +5.7% unrealized at 59.0x fwd P/E; Scotiabank downgraded as 'AI data center boom already priced in'; -0.6% from 52W high — this is stretched valuation territory for what is essentially a REIT growing revenue 8% annually; considering trim. (4) CIEN: +23.0% unrealized, +27.3% 1W at -1.1% from 52W high at 59.8x fwd P/E — 4 shares is already a small position; this has run enormously; at near-52W highs I should trim to bank gains. (5) GLW: +11.6% unrealized, +21.4% 1W at -0.1% from 52W high — the Zacks upgrade 'Corning Now Overvalued After Recent Share Price Momentum' and Simply Wall St. 'Is Corning Now Overvalued' signal valuation stretch; UBS commentary noted; at essentially the 52W high with fwd P/E 42.1x, trimming makes sense to lock in gains. (6) ARM: +3.3% unrealized, Morgan Stanley downgrade still weighing, -1.6% 1W — the thesis remains (AI silicon royalties) but at 69.6x fwd P/E with a fresh downgrade, 49 shares is still meaningful. (7) PANW: +3.7% unrealized, +8.4% 1W on Anthropic partnership at 43.8x fwd P/E — target 3-4% weight; currently at ~2.4% (15 shares x $173.78 = $2,607/$108K = ~2.4%); want to add to reach target. Action plan: SELL 10 CVX to reduce energy exposure as ceasefire removes risk premium and stop approaches; SELL 2 CIEN to bank gains at near-52W high with stretched 59.8x fwd P/E; SELL 5 GLW to bank gains at 52W high with 'overvalued' signals; BUY 5 PANW to build toward 3-4% target weight. The CVX proceeds fund PANW add. **Cash:** Cash at $5,594 (~5.1%) is adequate — the trades today are primarily defensive (reducing energy, trimming extended optical plays) and offensive (adding PANW on Anthropic partnership thesis), keeping cash near the same level post-trades.

**Macro:** The market continues to rally strongly — SPY +3.8% 1W, QQQ +4.4% 1W, SMH +9.8% 1W — with AI infrastructure leading the charge. Trump has issued new Hormuz threats before Iran talks, creating some energy uncertainty, but the broader risk-on environment from last week's ceasefire optimism is intact. The AI capex supercycle remains the dominant structural force with chip stocks at or near record highs (Barron's notes 'chip stocks at record highs' with a hint of caution). **Sectors:** Semiconductors are the standout — MRVL +28.5% 1M, KLAC +18.9% 1M, LRCX +20.2% 1M, AMAT +15% 1M, all near 52W highs; the BofA $1.3T chip revenue forecast continues to drive institutional positioning. Optical networking remains extended (CIEN +44.5% 1M, GLW +24.6% 1M, VIAV +43.8% 1M). Cybersecurity is bifurcating — Barron's reports 'CrowdStrike, Other Cybersecurity Stocks Get Crushed' as the Anthropic bump didn't last, with ZS and NOW both heavily shorted by the swing trader and declining. Energy is mixed with new Hormuz threats potentially reversing the ceasefire trade. **Positions:** TSM EARNINGS ALERT — reports April 16. I hold 9 shares at $341.49 (+7.0% unrealized). I am HOLDING through earnings: TSM is irreplaceable in the AI chip supply chain, Q1 CoWoS/packaging demand from NVDA/AVGO/AMD will be the key metric, Motley Fool calls it 'Best Trillion-Dollar Stock to Buy Right Now,' and Simply Wall St. asks 'Is it too late after 133% surge?' which suggests the market hasn't fully capitalized on the thesis. Citi is bullish. The 20.2x fwd P/E for 45% margins and 20% revenue growth is genuinely attractive. I will hold all 9 shares and consider adding on any post-earnings weakness if guidance is strong. Key position reviews: (1) MRVL at +26.4% unrealized and -1.7% from 52W high with 45 shares — Barron's just ran 'Marvell Stock Gets an Upgrade. Forget AI Chips, Focus on This.' suggesting the thesis is broadening but the stock is extremely extended; insider selling remains a watch item; the swing trader holds 60 shares confirming momentum but also crowding risk. At this price, the risk/reward is asymmetric to the downside — I should trim partially to lock in the +26.4% gain. (2) CVX at -9.8% unrealized and new Hormuz threats creating uncertainty — Iran situation flip-flopping; -3.6% 1W; stop at $185.81 is only $4.55 below current $190.36; the thesis (FCF generation, fortress balance sheet) remains but the near-term energy trade is highly uncertain; I'll hold with stop intact.
